Manchester United have offered Juan Mata to Inter Milan as part of a deal to bring in Joao Mario, according to reports in Italy.

The Spaniard started the season well under Jose Mourinho but has since fallen down the pecking order at Old Trafford.

And according to Italian paper Corriere dello Sport, United are using Mata as a makeweight in a move for Inter star Mario, with the permission of Mourinho.

However, the Italian publication claim that the Serie A outfit are not interested in signing the 29-year-old, but could be willing to negotiate a deal that includes Armenian midfielder Henrikh Mkhitaryan.

United could face stiff competition from Paris Saint-Germain for the signature of Mario as the Ligue 1 giants are also said to be interested in him.

馬達在最近表示自己很高興留在曼聯，但是他與球隊的合同將在本賽季結束後到期。

而據《羅馬體育報》消息，國際米蘭希望用馬里奧來直接交換馬達，雖然曼聯主帥摩連奴並不反對引進馬里奧，但是他並不想失去馬達。

報道還聲稱，摩連奴更希望國際米蘭以球員加錢的方式完成交易，而米希達恩似乎更有可能是去國際米蘭的球員。

今年29歲的馬達是在2014年冬季轉會窗從車路士加盟曼聯的，他已經在本賽季的英超聯賽出場了10次，打進1球，並送出1次助攻，此外他分別在歐聯小組賽和聯賽盃出場了4次和1次。

今年24歲的馬里奧則是在去年夏天從葡萄牙體育加盟國際米蘭的，他在本賽季的意甲聯賽只得到了4次首發出場的機會。

【now.com體育】輸得好！就算好似十三咁唔係曼城粉絲，都未必會對哥迪奧拿反感，尤其係佢係言談作風向來反映佢個性沉穩而城府深，而佢從來正面睇輸波呢樣，真係幾高。曼城咁輸一輸，過過冷河，要擔心嘅，應該係曼聯領隊摩連奴。
撰文：球十三

「當我覺得我哋需要輸，咁係為咗球員同埋球會。」哥迪奧拿賽後講得好清楚，曼城以1:2輸俾薩克達，老實，既然一早出線實在唔值得浪費兵力追求咩全勝，唔係話想特登輸，但球員心繫星期日曼徹斯特打吡合情合理。果然今場波曼城上半場了無生氣，被轟入兩球，但易邊後又踢番氣勢，不過相信喺嗰刻，藍月球迷只求唔好增添傷兵，而話唔定哥帥當時甚至想剎停班球員，追平與否，好次要啦。

哥帥執教曼城以嚟從未試過正式比賽連敗。輸一場關係唔大嘅比賽，冇咗咩連勝或者不敗包袱，等球員唔好太心紅，留番度氣同宿敵死過，唔係壞事。

「依家睇我哋周日會作咩反應，但我見到當我哋落後0:2嘅時候，過渡得好好。」哥迪奧拿城府深，打吡當前，點會錯過為大軍減壓嘅機會？「無論發生咩事，世界唔會喺星期日完結，依家我哋會準備嗰場比賽。」

睇曼城下半場稍為「按掣」即有料到根本冇有怕，家陣又唔需要再背負咩包袱準備降「魔」……

如果因為227日以嚟首敗，換嚟一場能夠打贏宿敵嘅大勝，除笨有精囉。

「依家睇我哋周日會作咩反應，但我見到當我哋落後0:2嘅時候，過渡得好好。」哥迪奧拿城府深，打吡當前，點會錯過為大軍減壓嘅機會？「無論發生咩事，世界唔會喺星期日完結，依家我哋會準備嗰場比賽。」

睇曼城下半場稍為「按掣」即有料到根本冇有怕，家陣又唔需要再背負咩包袱準備降「魔」……

如果因為227日以嚟首敗，換嚟一場能夠打贏宿敵嘅大勝，除笨有精囉。

Manchester United’s 13-year run of 58 televised FA Cup matches has come to an end, with their clash with Derby County next month a shock omission from the third-round games selected for broadcast.

Jose Mourinho gives green light for Man Utd to sign £95m midfielder

Metro Sport Reporter for Metro.co.uk
Saturday 9 Dec 2017 11:17 pm
Jose Mourinho gives green light for Man Utd to sign £95m midfielder
Sergej Milinkovic-Savic is Manchester United’s top target for next summer. (Getty Images)
Latest from Metro.co.uk»

Jose Mourinho has given the green light for Manchester United to make a £95 million bid for Lazio midfielder Sergej Milinkovic-Savic, according to reports.

The 22-year-old has been extensively scouted by United but is also attracting interest from Manchester City and Liverpool.

According to the Sunday Express, Mourinho has made the decision to push ahead with a move for the Serbia international next summer.

And United are ready to break their own transfer record with a £95 million bid.

Milinkovic-Savic has been tipped as a potential replacement for Marouane Fellaini, who could leave Old Trafford on a free transfer next summer if he fails to sign a new contract with United.

Jose Mourinho gives green light for Man Utd to sign £95m midfielder
Milinkovic Savic is valued at £100m by Lazio. (Getty Images)
Earlier this week, Milinkovic-Savic’s agent Mateja Kezman insisted that the midfielder would not be sold by Lazio.

‘The only person who can act on behalf of Sergej Milinkovic-Savic is myself,’ former Chelsea striker told TMW.

‘Therefore, what I’ve read is wrong in more than one way and puts unfair pressure on Lazio as it could disrupt the perfect balance in their dressing room, in what has been a magnificent season for both him and the club.

‘Has an offer from United actually arrived? Like I’ve said a million times already, Milinkovic-Savic is currently not for sale at any price.’

Jose Mourinho gives green light for Man Utd to sign £95m midfielder

Metro Sport Reporter for http://Metro.co.uk
Saturday 9 Dec 2017 11:17 pm
Jose Mourinho gives green light for Man Utd to sign £95m midfielder
Sergej Milinkovic-Savic is Manchester United’s top target for next summer. (Getty Images)
Latest from http://Metro.co.uk»

Jose Mourinho has given the green light for Manchester United to make a £95 million bid for Lazio midfielder Sergej Milinkovic-Savic, according to reports.

The 22-year-old has been extensively scouted by United but is also attracting interest from Manchester City and Liverpool.

According to the Sunday Express, Mourinho has made the decision to push ahead with a move for the Serbia international next summer.

And United are ready to break their own transfer record with a £95 million bid.

Milinkovic-Savic has been tipped as a potential replacement for Marouane Fellaini, who could leave Old Trafford on a free transfer next summer if he fails to sign a new contract with United.

Jose Mourinho gives green light for Man Utd to sign £95m midfielder
Milinkovic Savic is valued at £100m by Lazio. (Getty Images)
Earlier this week, Milinkovic-Savic’s agent Mateja Kezman insisted that the midfielder would not be sold by Lazio.

‘The only person who can act on behalf of Sergej Milinkovic-Savic is myself,’ former Chelsea striker told TMW.

‘Therefore, what I’ve read is wrong in more than one way and puts unfair pressure on Lazio as it could disrupt the perfect balance in their dressing room, in what has been a magnificent season for both him and the club.

‘Has an offer from United actually arrived? Like I’ve said a million times already, Milinkovic-Savic is currently not for sale at any price.’

http://www.telegraph.co.uk/football/2017/12/09/pep-guardiola-could-have-ended-man-utd-manager-had-understood/

Pep Guardiola has admitted he might have ended up managing Manchester United had he realised Sir Alex Ferguson was trying to line him up as his Old Trafford successor over dinner in New York

Ferguson claimed in his book, Leading, that he asked Guardiola, who was halfway through a 12-month sabbatical after leaving Barcelona, to contact him before he accepted an offer from another club. But the Catalan says he failed to grasp Ferguson’s wish and instead joined Bayern Munich, with United eventually turning to David Moyes.

“My English in that period was not as good as it is right now – and so maybe I just didn't understand what Sir Alex said,” said Guardiola.

“It was just two friends, two colleagues in football, talking about many things. But what I do remember is that Sir Alex spoke really fast and it was difficult to understand him! But it was nice because he chose an amazing restaurant – and of course he paid!”

“It was just two friends, two colleagues in football, talking about many things. But what I do remember is that Sir Alex spoke really fast and it was difficult to understand him! But it was nice because he chose an amazing restaurant – and of course he paid!”
